summary:
• Escalating Middle East geopolitical tensions, including U.S. military buildup, Iran sanctions enforcement, and risk of wider conflict, pose direct threats to global crude supply with potential multi-year energy crisis implications for Western markets.

• Venezuela's oil field restart agreement with Eni and ongoing OPEC+ production decisions represent critical Western Hemisphere supply wildcards, with medium-term uncertainty around coalition cohesion and member compliance affecting regional crude availability.

• Tightening U.S. crude inventories and surging refining margins are masking underlying supply vulnerability, as sustained geopolitical disruptions to Middle Eastern and Russian production could rapidly deplete strategic reserves and pressure Western Hemisphere refiners.
